title: IBM and Google Cloud launch joint enterprise AI delivery practice
subtitle: The new Google Cloud Practice pairs IBM Consulting Advantage with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to ship industry-specific agents across banking, government, retail, and life sciences.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: IBM and Google Cloud announced a strategic partnership on June 4, 2026, creating a new Google Cloud Practice inside IBM Consulting. The practice pairs thousands of Google Cloud-certified IBM consultants and forward-deployed engineers with Google's Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, cybersecurity stack, and data tooling. IBM will also build a portfolio of industry-specific AI agents — for banking, government, retail, telecommunications, energy, insurance, life sciences, and security — that run on Gemini Enterprise and IBM's own Consulting Advantage platform.

The deal targets a specific gap in enterprise AI: the distance between a working demo and a deployed system that actually changes how a bank approves loans or a hospital schedules surgeries. That gap is mostly consulting work — integration, governance, change management, security review — and IBM has spent decades building that muscle. Google has the model and the cloud; IBM has the people who can put both into a regulated environment without breaking compliance. The companies describe the opportunity as multi-billion-dollar, though neither broke out specific revenue commitments.

The announcement also signals where the agent platform race is settling. Microsoft has Agent 365 and Copilot. Anthropic has its Partner Network and Mythos. Google had been weaker on the system-integrator side of enterprise AI compared with the others; pairing with IBM closes that gap quickly and gives Gemini Enterprise the kind of distribution Azure has gotten from Accenture and Deloitte deals over the last eighteen months.

A note for learners: enterprise AI rollouts succeed or fail on integration, not model quality. The teams shipping real systems right now are the ones who can wire an agent into Salesforce, Workday, an EHR, and a custom ERP without breaking audit logs. If you're early in your career and want leverage in this market, the skill that pays is system integration — knowing how to make AI work inside the messy software a real business already runs.

sources.title: IBM and Google Cloud Announce Strategic Partnership to Scale AI — IBM Newsroom
